county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Genesee and Ontario Counties in 1821. Rochester is county seat and third-largest city in NY. Erie Canal transformed the city. Kodak, Xerox, Bausch and Lomb headquarters. Strong abolition heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: Monroe County Clerk in Rochester holds deed records from 1821 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Rochester was a boom town after the Erie Canal (1825) and records are extensive. The Rochester Public Library Local History Division has outstanding genealogical collections including city directories from 1827, German and Italian newspaper archives, and thousands of family files. The Rochester Museum and Science Center (RMSC) has manuscript collections. For African American research, Frederick Douglass lived in Rochester (1847-1872) and AME Zion Church records document the Black community. The Rundel Memorial Library has naturalization indexes. For German immigration, the German families settled in Rochester's northeast side and St. Joseph's, St. Boniface, and other ethnic parish records are at the Diocese of Rochester. Susan B. Anthony's papers and the women's suffrage movement records connect many Rochester families.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1821; online access through county clerk

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation.
Wills, administrations from 1821.
Declarations and petitions from Rochester and Monroe County courts. Large immigrant populations.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent. Indexed at Rochester Public Library.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1830.
Catholic parish records for German, Irish, Italian immigrant congregations.
Birth and death from statewide registration. Rochester city records earlier.
Rochester Democrat and Chronicle, Frederick Douglass' North Star.

county_metadata.record_losses: No significant losses. Records well preserved from formation.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Originally Tryon County (1772), renamed Montgomery in 1784. Mohawk Valley county. Named for Gen. Richard Montgomery. Sir William Johnson's territory. Erie Canal passed through.
county_metadata.research_tips: Montgomery County Clerk in Fonda holds deed records from 1772 (as Tryon County) and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same era. This is one of the most historically significant counties in NY — originally Tryon County encompassing all of NY west of the existing counties. Sir William Johnson's estate records (Johnson Hall in Johnstown) are essential for colonial Mohawk Valley research. The Montgomery County Dept of History and Archives in Fonda has genealogical files and cemetery records. The Mohawk Valley was a major Revolutionary War battleground and military records document local Patriot and Loyalist families. Dutch and Palatine German church records are critical — Fort Plain Reformed Church, Stone Arabia churches. The Erie Canal transformed Fonda, Amsterdam, and Fort Plain. Amsterdam attracted carpet mill workers (Mohawk Carpet, Bigelow-Sanford) from many immigrant groups.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1772 (as Tryon County); county clerk Fonda

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from 1772 (as Tryon County).
Wills, administrations from colonial era.

source_profiles.source_name: Palatine German Church Records — Mohawk Valley
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Palatine German Reformed and Lutheran records from Stone Arabia, Fort Plain, Palatine Bridge.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival. Many published and microfilmed.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1790. Earliest cover vast Tryon territory.
Revolutionary War records. Battle of Oriskany, Cherry Valley, and Mohawk Valley campaigns.
Amsterdam Recorder, Fonda Eagle, Canajoharie Courier.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Queens County in 1899 when Queens became a NYC borough. Long Island suburban county. One of wealthiest counties in US. Major suburban development after WWII.
county_metadata.research_tips: Nassau County Clerk in Mineola holds deed records from 1899. Pre-1899 records are at the Queens County Clerk in Jamaica. The Nassau County Division of Museum Services and the Long Island Studies Institute at Hofstra University have extensive genealogical collections. For colonial-era families, the original Long Island Dutch and English settlement records are in Queens County or at the NYPL and NY Historical Society. The Nassau County Historical Society maintains family files. Hempstead Town records go back to 1644 and are among the oldest on Long Island. Oyster Bay Historical Society has Theodore Roosevelt-related records. The Gold Coast mansions (Great Gatsby era) generated estate records. For 20th-century suburban genealogy, Levittown development records and post-WWII community documentation are at Hofstra. African American community records for Roosevelt and Freeport at local historical societies.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1899; pre-1899 at Queens County

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation. Pre-1899 at Queens County.
Wills, administrations from 1899. Pre-1899 at Queens.
Federal census from 1900 (first as separate county). Pre-1899 under Queens.

source_profiles.repository: NYPL / Long Island Studies Institute, Hofstra
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Colonial Dutch Reformed and Quaker records from Hempstead, Oyster Bay areas.
Birth and death from statewide registration.
Hempstead Sentinel, Nassau Daily Review-Star, Long Island Press.

county_metadata.record_losses: No significant losses. Outstanding record preservation from Dutch colonial period.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: One of the original 12 counties (1683). Coterminous with Borough of Manhattan (NYC consolidated 1898). The most important single county in American genealogy due to immigration through the Port of New York.
county_metadata.research_tips: New York County (Manhattan) is the most genealogically significant county in America. The NYC Municipal Archives at 31 Chambers Street holds birth (1866+), death (1795+), and marriage records (1866+). The New York County Clerk has deeds from 1654 (Dutch era). The New York Public Library Milstein Division at 42nd Street is one of the world's great genealogy libraries. The New York Genealogical and Biographical Society (NYG&B) on East 58th Street has indexed millions of NYC records. The NY Historical Society on Central Park West has manuscript collections from the Dutch period forward. For immigration, Castle Garden (1855-1890) and Ellis Island (1892-1954) records are online. The Holland Society of New York has published Dutch colonial church records. Five Points neighborhood records, Tammany Hall archives, and ward-level police records at Municipal Archives document immigrant neighborhoods.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1654 Dutch era; oldest in NY

source_profiles.source_name: New York County Clerk Deed Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from Dutch colonial period. Oldest continuous records in NY.
Birth certificates from 1866. Post-1909 from NYC Health Dept. Pre-1866 in church records.

source_profiles.source_name: Castle Garden and Ellis Island Records
source_profiles.repository: Statue of Liberty-Ellis Island Foundation / NARA
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Castle Garden (1855-1890) and Ellis Island (1892-1954) passenger records. The primary gateway for US immigration.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent. Fully digitized and searchable online.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations from Dutch colonial era.
Collegiate Reformed Church of NYC records from 1639. Published by Holland Society.
Federal census from 1790. Manhattan wards critical for tracking immigrant neighborhoods.
NY Times, Herald, Tribune, Sun, World. Immigrant press in dozens of languages.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Genesee County in 1808. Niagara Falls tourism and hydroelectric power. Erie Canal passed through Lockport. Border county with Canada.
county_metadata.research_tips: Niagara County Clerk in Lockport holds deed records from 1808 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1808 records in Genesee County. The Erie Canal locks at Lockport (1825) are iconic and canal worker records at NYS Archives document construction crews. The Niagara County Historical Society in Lockport has genealogical files. Niagara Falls tourism industry generated hotel and employment records from the 1830s. The Underground Railroad was active here with crossings to Canada at Lewiston and Niagara Falls. The Tuscarora Indian Reservation is in Niagara County and tribal records are maintained separately. The Old Fort Niagara archives have colonial and Revolutionary War records. Niagara University archives have Catholic education records. The chemical and power industries (Hooker Chemical, Love Canal) created 20th-century employment records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1808; county clerk Lockport

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation.
Wills, administrations from 1808.
Colonial, Revolutionary War, War of 1812 records at Fort Niagara.
Federal census from 1810.
Birth and death from statewide registration.
Lockport Union-Sun and Journal, Niagara Falls Gazette.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Herkimer County in 1798. Central NY. Utica and Rome are major cities. Erie Canal passed through Rome. Fort Stanwix (Rome) was key Revolutionary War site. Oneida Nation territory.
county_metadata.research_tips: Oneida County Clerk in Utica holds deed records from 1798 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1798 records in Herkimer County. Utica was a major canal city and later textile manufacturing center attracting Welsh, Irish, Italian, Polish, and later Bosnian immigrants. The Oneida County Historical Society in Utica has outstanding genealogical collections. Fort Stanwix National Monument (Rome) has Revolutionary War records. The Oneida Nation territory is within the county and tribal records are separate. Utica Public Library has city directories from 1817 and ethnic newspaper archives. The Welsh settlement in Utica (1790s-1850s) is well documented in Moriah Church records. Rome Historical Society has Erie Canal construction records. Griffiss Air Force Base (former) records at NARA. The Munson-Williams-Proctor Arts Institute has family manuscripts of industrial-era Utica.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1798; county clerk Utica

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation.
Wills, administrations from 1798.
Declarations and petitions from Utica and Rome courts. Large immigrant populations.
Federal census from 1800.
Birth and death from statewide registration.
Revolutionary War records from Fort Stanwix. Battle of Oriskany nearby.
Utica Observer-Dispatch, Rome Daily Sentinel.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Herkimer County in 1794. Syracuse is county seat and major salt industry/canal city. Onondaga Nation capital of Iroquois Confederacy located here. Syracuse University.
county_metadata.research_tips: Onondaga County Clerk in Syracuse holds deed records from 1794 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1794 records in Herkimer County. Syracuse was the 'Salt City' and salt industry records at the Onondaga Historical Association (OHA) document workers and investors. The Onondaga Nation is the capital of the Haudenosaunee (Iroquois Confederacy) and maintains its own records separate from the county. OHA in Syracuse has excellent genealogical collections including family files, city directories from 1844, and Syracuse University Special Collections has manuscripts. Erie Canal records for the Syracuse section are at NYS Archives. For immigrant research, the north side Italian community records and the Jewish community records (Temple Adath Yeshurun from 1839) are significant. The Levi family (who built the first salt works) papers are at OHA.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1794; county clerk Syracuse

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation. Salt industry land transactions significant.
Wills, administrations from 1794.
Federal census from 1800. Syracuse manufacturing schedules valuable.
Naturalization records from Syracuse courts. Diverse immigrant population.
Birth and death from statewide registration.
Syracuse Post-Standard, Syracuse Herald-Journal.

county_metadata.record_losses: No significant losses. Records well preserved.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Montgomery County in 1789. Finger Lakes region. Canandaigua was site of Seneca treaty (1794). One of the earliest western NY counties. Originally very large territory.
county_metadata.research_tips: Ontario County Clerk in Canandaigua holds deed records from 1789 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Ontario County was originally vast, encompassing most of western NY, so early records here relate to a huge area. The Ontario County Historical Society in Canandaigua has genealogical files and Phelps and Gorham Purchase records documenting original land sales. Canandaigua was where the Treaty of Canandaigua (1794) with the Seneca Nation was signed. The Granger Homestead Society has records of the Granger family and local elite. For Latter-day Saints research, the Hill Cumorah area and Palmyra (Wayne County, but Ontario County connections) are significant. The county's Finger Lakes wine industry generated vineyard and agricultural records. Victor was a major Seneca settlement (Ganondagan) and the Ganondagan State Historic Site documents pre-contact Seneca history.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1789; county clerk Canandaigua

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation. Phelps-Gorham Purchase deeds critical.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival at Historical Society and State Archives.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: One of the original 12 counties (1683). Lower Hudson Valley. West Point Military Academy located here. Goshen is county seat. Newburgh was Washington's headquarters 1782-1783.
county_metadata.research_tips: Orange County Clerk in Goshen holds deed records from 1703 (earliest surviving) and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same era. As an original county, Orange has deep colonial records. Washington's Headquarters State Historic Site in Newburgh has Revolutionary War records for the Continental Army period. The Orange County Historical Society has genealogical files. West Point archives have military academy records from 1802. For Dutch and Huguenot families, New Paltz (Ulster, but bordering) and Goshen area Reformed Church records are essential. The Minisink area along the Delaware River has some of the oldest European settlement records in the mid-Hudson. The Chester, Monroe, and Warwick historical societies have local records. For 20th century, Stewart Air Force Base and Camp Shanks (WWII embarkation point) records are at NARA.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1703; county clerk Goshen

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from colonial era.
Wills, administrations from late colonial period.
Continental Army records from Washington's final headquarters at Newburgh.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good preservation as state historic site.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1790.
